title: Narrative Furnace

fields.vision: Narrative Furnace is a proposed MuranoOS-native governed room that behaves less like a notes app and more like a "story war cell" for constructing compelling, gritty, morally gray, high-pressure dystopian stories.
fields.mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention

fields.source_utilization: Writing, rhetoric, narrative design, visual culture, and presentation. Use source library writing/visual/cinema sources to strengthen public exposition and Trilogy visual/storytelling logic. Vision: Make public pages read as curated intellectual exhibits rather than dashboards. Mechanism: Extract narrative sequencing, visual perception, close reading, rhetoric, style, cinema, and presentation mechanisms. Plan: Apply after domain-source routing so style clarifies evidence rather than masking weak evidence. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need selective close-reading before adopting style doctrine. Source pool: 64 deduplicated local title candidates; 61 catalogue support rows. Examples: Narrative and Narration: Analyzing Cinematic Storytelling; Camera Lucida. Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact project-method and creativity passages. Source pool: 151 deduplicated local title candidates; 154 catalogue support rows. Examples: Creativity Research; The Heritage, Creativity and Innovation Nexus. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. Read 00_Overview/README.md — consume 02_Canon/ trio to lock doctrine — cross-check 01_Reviews/CANON_VS_LIVE_REPO_DELTA_MAP.md to know what's live vs proposal — implement tranche defined in 01_Reviews/NEXT_HIGHEST_VALUE_IMPLEMENTATION_TRANCHE.md using 04_Data/ schema and 05_Build_Packets/ stubs. Wire or preserve the implementation files: 05_Build_Packets/ contains stubs: NarrativeFurnaceRoom.tsx, narrativeFurnaceRuntime.ts, features/narrative-furnace/routes.tsx, src-tauri/src/narrative_furnace_cmds.rs — Phase I shell scaffolding, not the full 15-tool suite.

fields.detailed_source_vision: Narrative Furnace is a proposed MuranoOS-native governed room that behaves less like a notes app and more like a "story war cell" for constructing compelling, gritty, morally gray, high-pressure dystopian stories. It is explicitly engineered to preserve plot propulsion, factional conflict, intrigue, mystery, violence-with-consequence, state-terror textures, disease/decay atmospherics, philosophical conflict embodied in action, and continuous anti-boring red-team review. The pack distinguishes two products: an internal product (the Narrative Furnace Room — persistent, project-based, allows ugly internal complexity: raw scaffolding, contradictions, wounds, thresholds, faction mechanics, scenes, secrets, diagnostics) and an external derived export layer (public-safe story bible summaries, presentation-quality world design packets, sanitized portfolio views — no raw internal notes unless intentionally released). The governing rule is explicit: this pack...

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: blueprint detailed source fields.

Source context: Narrative Furnace is a proposed MuranoOS-native governed room that behaves less like a notes app and more like a "story war cell" for constructing compelling, gritty, morally gray, high-pressure dystopian stories. It is explicitly engineered to preserve plot propulsion, factional conflict, intrigue, mystery, violence-with-consequence, state-terror textures, disease/decay atmospherics, philosophical conflict embodied.
instruments.output: a project-specific work product, decision, or proof surface tied to `Story War Cell`
instruments.upstream_dependency: Writing, rhetoric, narrative design, visual culture, and presentation. Use source library writing/visual/cinema sources to strengthen public exposition and Trilogy visual/storytelling logic. Vision: Make public pages read as curated intellectual exhibits.
instruments.downstream_use: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. Read 00_Overview/README.md — consume 02_Canon/ trio to lock doctrine — cross-check 01_Reviews/CANON_VS_LIVE_REPO_DELTA_MAP.md to know what's live vs proposal —.
instruments.proof: Narrative Furnace is a proposed MuranoOS-native governed room that behaves less like a notes app and more like a "story war cell" for constructing compelling, gritty, morally gray, high-pressure dystopian stories. It is explicitly engineered to preserve plot.
instruments.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
instruments.gap: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved.
instruments.verification_check: Confirm `Story War Cell` appears in the cited source context and that the public page explains what output, decision, or project function it supports.
